arts.ttl: Thomas Frank confirms major injury blow ahead of Aston Villa clash
arts.sum: Brentford head coach Thomas Frank confirms a major injury blow ahead of Wednesday's Premier League clash with Aston Villa at Villa Park.

arts.ttl: Team News: Aston Villa vs. Brentford injury, suspension list, predicted XIs
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest injury and suspension news ahead of Wednesday's Premier League clash between Aston Villa and Brentford.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa injury news as update delivered on duo ahead of Brentford
arts.sum: Unai Emery confirmed Amadou Onana will not feature against Brentford as he continues to nurse a foot injury, while Jacob Ramsey is expected to miss at least another two weeks of action. “We are going to train this afternoon and I don’t know how he’s going to feel,” said the Villa manager.

arts.ttl: Former Aston Villa, Liverpool and Crystal Palace striker makes MLS team of 2024 alongside Lionel Messi
arts.sum: The MLS have announced their Best XI of 2024, with former Red Devils Christian Benteke joining Lionel Messi as one of the forwards. The now 34-year-old has had a stellar…

arts.ttl: Aston Villa's transfer window plans and latest on Emi Martinez injury
arts.sum: Hopefully tomorrow is a good challenge and a good day to recover scoring, playing well and trying to impose and dominate against the opponent. Unai Emery sat down with the media at Bodymoor Heath ahead of Aston Villa's Premier League clash against Brentford.

arts.ttl: Matchday Guide: Aston Villa v Brentford
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Brentford - Wed, Dec 4th 2024, 20:15 Aston Villa welcome Brentford to B6 on Wednesday night for the club's first midweek Premier League fixture of the season. Unai Emery's side entertain the Bees at Villa Park aiming to get back to winning ways Here's everything you need to know ahead of the encounter to enjoy your visit on matchday... Rainbow Laces Fixture null Rainbow Laces Fixture Aston Villa will once again demonstrate the club’s ongoing support for LGBTQ+ inclusion by championing Stonewall’s Rainbow Laces campaign at Wednesday’s game. Every top flight fixture between November 29 and December 5 is dedicated to celebrating the ongoing commitment to inclusion and support for LGBTQ+ people in football. Rainbow armbands will be worn by captains, while rainbow flags, ball plinths, handshake boards, LED perimeter boards and more will highlight support and demonstrate that football is everyone's game at Villa Park, in a variety of ways. Read full details here. Getting to the game Click on the sections below to learn more about tickets, Villa Park and transport to the stadium. Mobile Tickets Villa Park is now fully NFC ticketing enabled for supporters to access the stadium. Near-Field Communication (NFC) enables fans to enter the stadium via Apple or Google Wallet. Supporters can now also access their match-by-match tickets and enter the stadium directly via the Aston Villa app. Simply log into your Digital Account in the app and your tickets will appear under the ‘Tickets’ section. Supporters must update their app to the latest version to ensure in-app ticketing works without issue. Tickets A limited number of general admission tickets for the match remain. Book online here. The best way to secure tickets for future matches at Villa Park is via a Membership. Get Matchday Ready Ahead of the match at Villa Park, the club would like to advise supporters of the following ticketing information to ensure everyone enjoys their visit to our stadium. Fans will need to provide one of the two specified ticket types to gain entry to the stadium - access tickets in our official Aston Villa mobile app or download to Apple/Google Wallet for NFC entry. Hospitality and Premium Experiences The club has a range of new hospitality and GA+ areas to complement our existing offer. Check out all the information and book online here. Hospitality bookings for our upcoming home matches can be made over the phone on 0333 323 1982 (option 1) or by emailing hospitality@avfc.co.uk. Under-3s Policy Many supporters may not be aware of the policy regarding under-3s, so we are reminding supporters that under-3s are only permitted to be in the Family areas in the Trinity Road stand at Villa Park and must have a match ticket. Further information can be found here Aston Villa Ticketing (avfc.co.uk) Visiting Villa Park It's important that supporters take steps to plan their visit to the stadium. Turnstiles open at 6.45pm, so make sure you arrive at the stadium in plenty of time ahead of kick-off to ensure smooth entry. Please note, access to stiles is maintained during the first half and will close once the half-time whistle is blown. However you're planning on getting to the match, take a look at our Travel & Parking guide before leaving home. A traffic exclusion zone will be in operation around the stadium, meaning there will be less traffic on Trinity Road and Witton Lane from up to two hours before the match starts. The Holte Pub will open three hours prior to kick-off and close two hours after full-time. Parking and Travel Supporters are advised that the supply of car parking around Villa Park is limited. Fans can pre-book their space via either of the links below: www.astonvillaparking.co.uk/football/events www.countrywidemanagementsolutions.co.uk We will continue to work with local parking operators to offer fans the opportunity to book parking near the stadium. We would strongly encourage all supporters who have not pre-booked their car parking to travel to Villa Park by alternative modes. Covered cycle parking is available at our Inner City Academy on Witton Road. Plan ahead and check train timetables for direct services or travel earlier for less busy services. Visit Transport for West Midlands’ Journey Planner or the National Rail Enquiries real-time journey planner here. There are a number of Park and Ride sites across the region, for further details visit Park and Ride. To help you plan your journey by public transport, visit How to get to Villa Park. There is no planned disruption to key road, rail and bus routes to Villa Park on Wednesday. There is an amended late evening/early morning service between London Euston and Wolverhampton from Tuesday, December 3 to Friday, December 6, however this is currently unlikely to affect travel to and from Villa Park on Wednesday. The latest information on this is available here. Supporters are also advised that the city centre is likely to be busier than normal, as the Christmas Market is now live. City Centre and Tamworth Shuttle Buses Aston Villa is delighted to confirm to supporters that our AV82 city centre shuttle bus service will continue to run on matchdays this season. This follows a successful trial of the service at the back end of last season. The service will be in operation for all men’s home matches in the Premier League, UEFA Champions League and all domestic cup competitions, helping supporters travel more easily to and from Villa Park. Run by Stagecoach, the AV82 shuttle picks supporters up from outside Birmingham New Street Station on Dudley Street, running directly to Villa Park. Buses run every 15 minutes, starting three hours before kick-off for every home fixture. Services run back to Birmingham New Street at regular intervals for up to 90 minutes after the final whistle of every game, with services departing from Grosvenor Road, on the walking route towards Aston Station. The club also has a dedicated bus service, AV1, that runs to Villa Park from Tamworth on matchday. You can purchase tickets here. More information is available here. Cycle Parking Covered and secure cycle parking is available a short walk away from Villa Park at our Inner City Academy, Brook Vale, on Witton Road. Accessibility information Supporters with accessibility requirements travelling to Villa Park by rail are advised to look out for the accessibility signage on both platforms at Witton Station. Station staff will be able to help you if you wait in the designated areas. More information is available here. For any supporters with additional mobility needs who have questions about travel to and from Villa Park on a matchday, please contact accessibility@avfc.co.uk. Pre-match activities Click on the sections below to learn more about activities taking place at Villa Park before kick-off. Enjoy the Fan Zone Villa Park's new Fan Zone is again open on Wednesday, located in the former Players' Car Park. The new space opens its doors at 5.15pm, with supporters able to enjoy a bar area, food and big screen TVs. The Fan Zone is also in prime position to watch player arrivals from 6.45pm. We also have entertainment taking place on the stage opposite Trinity Reception, including live music. We want to see your experience of matchday at Villa Park! Post your photos and tag #UTV and you could be featured on the big screens at the stadium! Supporters are advised that the R44 accessible entrance will be closed for a period of time. Family Fun Zone Work is soon to begin on the transformation of the existing Academy building at Villa Park. Unfortunately, due to the planned works, the Academy building is not operational to the extent to which we can safely open the Family Fun Zone. The club are working on alternate arrangements to enable a continuation of some of the family-friendly pre-match activities. Updates will follow in the coming weeks. Food and Beverage Villa Park offers a wide range of cuisine and beverages on a matchday. Outside the stadium, several street food trucks will sit alongside a bar serving alcoholic and soft drinks from 12noon. Supporters are reminded that Aston Villa adopts a one-cup system at Villa Park, in our continued environmental sustainability efforts. Cashless Stadium Please note, Villa Park will be cashless to help ensure safe social distancing practices and minimise contact at all points of sale. This includes the Villa Store, with card payments widely accepted in and around the stadium. The only exception is programme kiosks outside the ground, which will still accept cash. In-stadia betting kiosks will also be in operation for this fixture and such service will be provided on a cashless basis. The Warehouse Design Update The Warehouse, which will now be 40,000 sqft, has been further developed with an enhanced design to accommodate live music and entertainment events for 3,500+ visitors and fans. Structural alterations to over half the rooftop will make way for a newly raised flat roof which will accommodate a generous mezzanine floor and viewing galley from above for both live music and fan zone entertainment. Creating a multi-use space called ‘The Warehouse’, will include a diverse range of Food & Beverage offers and pre- and post-match and event entertainment. The Warehouse will become a vibrant and ‘go to’ destination for the community embedded in the Villa Park and Witton Street scene. Matchday Programme The latest edition of the Villa News & Record will be available to purchase at Saturday's match! Fans can continue their sticker album collection, while the programme is also packed with features and news from around the club. Supporters will be able to purchase copies from sellers at Villa Park on matchday, while they're also available to purchase in the club shop and online here. Villa Store Click on the sections below to learn about merchandise points around the stadium. Store Opening Hours There are a number of places where supporters can shop for Aston Villa merchandise on a matchday, including the upgraded Villa Store. Below you'll find the opening times for each shop around the ground... Villa Store: 9.30am-10.30pm Holte Store: 5pm-8pm and 9.30pm-10.30pm 82 Lounge: 5pm-8pm and 8.30pm-10.30pm Lower Grounds: 5pm-8pm and 8.30pm-10.30pm Trinity Store: 6.30pm-end of half-time Click & Collect Don’t miss out on the matchday bundle. You can place your merch orders now and simply Click & Collect on matchday! Pre-order your items and collect at the Villa Store, Holte Store, Holte Suite or Hospitality 82 on matchday. Please note, the Click & Collect order option will be turned off at half-time, but any orders placed up to then will be available to collect until that retail unit closes. Click & Collect here. During the game Click on the sections below to learn more about events that take place during the match. See it...hear it...report it! If you witness discriminatory behaviour at a match or around a stadium, please report it to the police or a steward. Supporters at Villa Park can text REPORT to 60060 along with the location of the incident and details of what you have witnessed. The text line also includes the reporting of any safeguarding concerns. The text service will be supported by our security team and West Midlands Police. Each text costs one standard message at your network rate. The club may share your details with West Midlands Police if further information is required to assist with a criminal prosecution. All-seater Stadium We would like to remind supporters that Villa Park is an all-seater stadium. In all areas of Villa Park, we have children, elderly fans and those with medical conditions who are unable to see or watch the match if supporters stand up in front of them. Please be mindful of this at all times and thank you for your cooperation. Fan Engagement Click the sections below to learn about how you can get involved on matchday and our fan loyalty programme, Villa Rewards. Get Involved New for 2024, the ‘Villa Predictor’ game will give fans the opportunity to win a £500 store voucher if they correctly predict the outcome of the game. T&Cs apply. We want to know about your matchday experience at Villa Park. Tweet us using #UTV and you could feature on the big screens at the stadium! Pride Rewards The Pride Rewards Portal is back up and running. Log in here. AV150 Legacy Wall Aston Villa is excited to reveal a new AV150 Legacy Wall at Villa Park as part of our 150th anniversary celebrations. Supporters have the opportunity to become part of our iconic home, wherever you are in the world, by purchasing your very own personalised plaque at Villa Park. Our Legacy Wall is a place where you, your friends and family can show your support for the club, celebrate births, marriages, anniversaries or pay tribute to loved ones. For more information, click here.

arts.ttl: Premier League games on Amazon Prime TV this week including Aston Villa vs Brentford
arts.sum: Prime Video will also air 10 fixtures over the Christmas period on December 26 and 27, with notable clashes including Man City vs Everton, Liverpool vs Leicester and Arsenal vs Ipswich. Man City, the ailing Premier League champions, will host Nottingham Forest, while Chelsea are set to travel to Southampton.

arts.ttl: Brentford without six players for Aston Villa clash as Thomas Frank reveals fresh blow
arts.sum: Gustavo Nunes, Rico Henry, Aaron Hickey and Josh Dasilva remain sidelined for Brentford, who head to Villa Park winless away from home in the league this season. Meanwhile, defender Kristoffer Ajer has missed the last five Premier League fixtures due to a foot injury – and the midweek visit to Villa Park will come too soon for him.

arts.ttl: Unai Emery already knows where Aston Villa are going wrong and what has to change
arts.sum: Asked about Villa's lack of goals, Emery said: "The only way I know is to work even harder. Unai Emery expects Aston Villa to climb the table when they improve the "small details" which have let them down so far this season.

arts.ttl: Pepe Reina makes Aston Villa claim which will raise eyebrows at Liverpool
arts.sum: He also won the Italian Cup with Napoli and the Bundesliga with Bayern Munich, but staying up with Villa, at West Ham, in the summer of 2020 will remain among his best achievements. Former Aston Villa goalkeeper Pepe Reina has surprisingly revealed that, despite all that he has achieved in his decorated career, staying up during his short time at Villa Park was among his favourite memories in football.
